memoryArtifacts.headline: S J S gravestone found next to Sara Jane Sullivan's Gravestone in the Parker Sullivan Homestead Cemetery
memoryArtifacts.description:
Found next to Sarah Jane's gravestone in the cemetery.
S J S gravestone in 2 pieces on the garage floor ready to be mortared together prior to being reset in the cemetery on the hill above the house by Chris Brodie who restored the Parker Sullivan house in 2007 at 4193 Sullivan Road, West Liberty, Harrison Township, Champaign, Ohio

memoryArtifacts.headline: Parker Sullivan Homestead Cemetery
memoryArtifacts.description: Family cemetery on the hill above the house on Sullivan Road, Harrison Township, Champaign, Ohio.  13 gravestones in a 1 acre plot with Parker and Martha at the foot of the lilac.

memoryArtifacts.headline: ? Sullivan Gravestone in the Parker Sullivan Homestead Cemetery
memoryArtifacts.description: A gravestone in the Family cemetery on the hill above the house on Sullivan Road, Harrison Township, Champaign, Ohio. zh.lifeSummary: 當Parker Sullivan於1782年4月11日出生在美國新澤西州時，他的父親Captain Ebenezer Moses Sullivan31歲，母親Mary Parker24歲。他於1804年9月1日與Martha Mary Johns在美國新澤西州薩萊姆結婚。他們至少是4個兒子和3個女兒的父母。他於1850年住在美國俄亥俄州哈帕易恩哈里森 堂史普。他去世於1852年8月28日在美國俄亥俄州哈帕易恩享年70歲，埋葬在美國俄亥俄州哈帕易恩哈里森 堂史普。

nameMeanings.name: Sullivan
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Irish Brendan, Liam, Donal, James Patrick, Michael Patrick, Aileen, Brigid, Paddy, Siobhan, John Patrick, Kevin Patrick, Parnell.
Irish (Cork and Kerry): shortened form of   O'Sullivan  .

variants.name: O'Sullivan
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Irish Brendan, Donal, Liam, Siobhan, Declan, Kieran, Cathal, Dermot, Eamon, Fergus, Grainne, Seamus.
Irish (Munster): Anglicized form of Gaelic Ó Súileabháin ‘descendant of Súileabhán’, a personal name from súil ‘eye’ + dubh ‘dark’ + the diminutive suffix -án. Compare Sullivan .

nameMeanings.name: Parker
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Mainly U.S.: transferred use of the common surname, in origin an occupational name for a gamekeeper employed in a medieval game park.

historicEvents.title: A Free America
historicEvents.description: The Revolutionary War ended with the signing of the Treaty of Paris which gave the new nation boundries on which they could expand and trade with other countries without any problems.

historicEvents.title: New Jersey Plan
historicEvents.description: "Also referred to as the Small State Plan, the New Jersey Plan was an important piece of legislation that William Paterson presented during the Constitutional Convention. The plan was created because states with smaller populations were concerned about their representation in the United States government. The New Jersey plan proposed, among other things, that each state would have one equal vote. This was in contrast to the Virginia Plan, which suggested that appointment for Congress should be proportional to state population. The Connecticut Compromise merged the two plans, allowing for two ""houses"" of congress: one with proportional representation, and the other with equal power from each state (as the New Jersey Plan had suggested)."
Ohio was the first state admitted to the Union from the Northwest Territory.

Alexander Hamilton and Aaron Burr had been political enemies with intense personal differences for quite some time. Burr accused Hamilton of publicly disparaging his character during the elections of 1800 and 1804. On the morning of July 11, the two politicians went to Weehawken, New Jersey to resolve the disputes with an official duel. Both men were armed with a pistol. Hamilton missed, but Burr's shot fatally wounded Hamilton, who would die by the following day. The duel custom had been outlawed in New York by 1804, resulting in Burr fleeing the state due to an arrest warrant. He would later be accused of treason, but ultimately be acquitted.
historicEvents.urlTitle: Library of Congress - Today In History: July 11 (A Tragic Duel)

historicEvents.description: On January 28, 1820, the New Jersey Legislature incorporated the City of Jersey from parts of the Bergen Township. The city would be reincorporated two more times (January 23, 1829 and February 22, 1838) before receiving its official name. Jersey City became part of the new Hudson County in February of 1840.

historicEvents.title: Kirtland Temple Dedicated
historicEvents.description: On March 27, 1836, the Kirtland Temple was dedicated.

The 1844 revision of the New Jersey State Constitution made some significant changes. Suffrage rights were revoked from women and non-whites, meaning that only white men could vote. A separation of powers was established between executive, legislative, and judicial branches. A new bill of rights was provided, and the state now had the right to elect the governor.
U.S. acquires vast tracts of Mexican territory in wake of Mexican War including California and New Mexico.

en.lifeSummary: When Parker Sullivan was born on 11 April 1782, in New Jersey, United States, his father, Captain Ebenezer Moses Sullivan, was 31 and his mother, Mary Parker, was 24. He married Martha Mary Johns on 1 September 1804, in Salem, New Jersey, United States. They were the parents of at least 4 sons and 3 daughters. He lived in Harrison Township, Champaign, Ohio, United States in 1850. He died on 28 August 1852, in Champaign, Ohio, United States, at the age of 70, and was buried in Harrison Township, Champaign, Ohio, United States.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Parker Sullivan est né le 11 avril 1782, à New Jersey, États-Unis, son père, Captain Ebenezer Moses Sullivan, avait 31 ans et sa mère, Mary Parker, avait 24 ans. Il a épousé Martha Mary Johns le 1 septembre 1804, à Salem, New Jersey, États-Unis. Ils ont eu au moins 4 fils et 3 filles. Il a vécu à Harrison Township, Champaign, Ohio, États-Unis en 1850. Il est décédé le 28 août 1852, à Champaign, Ohio, États-Unis, à l’âge de 70 ans, et a été enterré à Harrison Township, Champaign, Ohio, États-Unis.

de.lifeSummary: Als Parker Sullivan am 11. April 1782 in New Jersey, Vereinigte Staaten geboren wurde, war sein Vater Captain Ebenezer Moses Sullivan 31 und seine Mutter Mary Parker 24. Er hat am 1. September 1804 in Salem, New Jersey, Vereinigte Staaten Martha Mary Johns geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 4 Söhne und 3 Töchter. 1850 hat er in Harrison Township, Champaign, Ohio, Vereinigte Staaten gelebt. Er starb am 28. August 1852 im Alter von 70 Jahren in Champaign, Ohio, Vereinigte Staaten und wurde in Harrison Township, Champaign, Ohio, Vereinigte Staaten begraben.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Parker Sullivan è nato il il 11 aprile 1782 nel New Jersey, Stati Uniti d'America, il suo padre, Captain Ebenezer Moses Sullivan, aveva 31 anni e il suo madre, Mary Parker, aveva 24 anni. Lui sposato Martha Mary Johns il 1 settembre 1804, nel Salem, New Jersey, Stati Uniti d'America. Erano genitori di almeno 4 figli e 3 figlie. Vissuto a Harrison Township, Champaign, Ohio, Stati Uniti d'America nel 1850. ja.lifeSummary: Parker Sullivanが1782年4月11日にアメリカ合衆国ニュージャージー州で生まれた時，父Captain Ebenezer Moses Sullivanは31歳，母Mary Parkerは24歳であった。1804年9月1日にアメリカ合衆国ニュージャージー州サレムでMartha Mary Johnsと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも4人の息子と3人の娘の親であった。彼は1850年にはアメリカ合衆国オハイオ州チャンペイグンハーリソン トウンシュイップに住んでいた。彼は1852年8月28日にアメリカ合衆国オハイオ州チャンペイグンで70歳で死去し，アメリカ合衆国オハイオ州チャンペイグンハーリソン トウンシュイップにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Parker Sullivan(이)가 1782년 4월 11일 미국 뉴저지 주에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Captain Ebenezer Moses Sullivan(은)는 31세였고, 그의 어머니 Mary Parker(은)는 24세였다. 그는 1804년 9월 1일 미국 뉴저지 주 살렘에서 Martha Mary Johns(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 네 명의 아들과 세 명의 딸을 두었다. 1850년도에 미국 오하이오 주 챔파익 해리슨 타운시프에서 살았다. 그는 1852년 8월 28일 미국 오하이오 주 챔파익에서 70세의 나이로 사망했으며 미국 오하이오 주 챔파익 해리슨 타운시프에 묻혔다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Parker Sullivan nasceu em 11 de abril de 1782, em Nova Jérsei, Estados Unidos da América, seu pai, Captain Ebenezer Moses Sullivan, tinha 31 anos e sua mãe, Mary Parker, tinha 24 anos. Ele casou-se com Martha Mary Johns em 1 de setembro de 1804, em Salem, Nova Jérsei, Estados Unidos da América. Eles tiveram pelo menos 4 filhos e 3 filhas. Ele viveu em Harrison Township, Champaign, Ohio, Estados Unidos da América em 1850. Ele faleceu em 28 de agosto de 1852, em Champaign, Ohio, Estados Unidos da América, com 70 anos, e foi sepultado em Harrison Township, Champaign, Ohio, Estados Unidos da América.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Parker Sullivan родился 11 апреля 1782 г. в местности Нью-Джерси, Соединенные штаты, его отцу по имени Captain Ebenezer Moses Sullivan было 31 год, а матери, которую звали Mary Parker, 24. Он женился на женщине по имени Martha Mary Johns 1 сентября 1804 г. в местности сеилем, Нью-Джерси, Соединенные штаты. У них было по крайней мере 4 сына и 3 дочери. Он жил в местности харрисон тоуншип, чампеигн, Огайо, Соединенные штаты в 1850 году. Он умер 28 августа 1852 г. в местности чампеигн, Огайо, Соединенные штаты в возрасте 70 лет и был похоронен в местности харрисон тоуншип, чампеигн, Огайо, Соединенные штаты.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Parker Sullivan nació el 11 de abril de 1782, en Nueva Jersey, Estados Unidos, su papá, Captain Ebenezer Moses Sullivan, tenía 31 años y su mamá, Mary Parker, tenía 24 años. Se casó con Martha Mary Johns el 1 de septiembre de 1804, en Salem, Nueva Jersey, Estados Unidos. Fueron padres de por lo menos 4 hijos y 3 hijas. Vivió en Harrison Township, Champaign, Ohio, Estados Unidos en 1850. Murió el 28 de agosto de 1852, en Champaign, Ohio, Estados Unidos, a la edad de 70 años, y fue sepultado en Harrison Township, Champaign, Ohio, Estados Unidos.
